phases.name: Countered-own-claims count surfaced on the front door + /claims header (thick walking skeleton + close confirmations)

steps.name: Wire count_countered_own_claims + the 4th LandingSummary field + render_countered + render_landing render + store-threading and show "12 own claims (3 countered)" on GET / (THICK walking skeleton)
steps.description: The thick foundational walking-skeleton step (CC-WS, US-CC-001 Theme 1 — the riskiest-assumption thread). It forces the WHOLE countered-count thread into existence end-to-end: viewer → the LOCAL countered-count aggregate → the extended LandingSummary → the shared pure render → the front-door summary showing the disputed-claim awareness count. (ports) Add `count_countered_own_claims(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>` to StoreReadPort — a read-only sibling of the slice-17 `count_active_peer_subscriptions` / `count_claims` / `count_peer_claims` (ADR-055 D1); NO mutation method added to the port (ADR-030 / C-1). (adapter-duckdb) Impl it as ONE aggregate over the SAME shared connection: `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.cid) FROM claims c WHERE c.cid IN (SELECT referenced_cid FROM claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters' UNION SELECT referenced_cid FROM peer_claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters')` — a PRESENCE count (the de-duped UNION IN-set + COUNT(DISTINCT) collapses a claim countered by N peers to ONE membership, no JOIN-fanout, ADR-055 D1), own-only by query shape (outer table is `claims`), parameter-free / injection-safe (literal `'counters'` only), invariant to store size (both ref columns indexed, slice-12 ADR-048); names `claims`/`claim_references`/`peer_claim_references` so the xtask `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` rule stays GREEN by construction (the `_` after `peer_claims` fails the whole-word boundary, ADR-055 Enforcement). (viewer-domain) Add a FOURTH `pub countered_own_claims: Option<usize>` field to `LandingSummary` (ADR-055 D2 — additive, parallel to the slice-17 three, identical degrade semantics) and a shared `pub fn render_countered(countered: Option<usize>) -> String` helper (ADR-055 D3): `Some(n) → "(n countered)"`, `Some(0) → "(0 countered)"` (honest zero), `None → "(— countered)"` (reusing the slice-17 `render_count` / MISSING_COUNT_MARKER inner-number mapping), the copy NEUTRAL disputed-claim awareness (never a verdict/penalty/"disputed by N", C-6). Extend `render_landing(&LandingSummary)` to render `render_countered(summary.countered_own_claims)` BESIDE the UNCHANGED own-claims line (the own-claims `render_count` "12" untouched — additive, never a re-weight, C-4) → "12 own claims (3 countered)". The pure render stays a TOTAL function of LandingSummary (now 2⁴ Option combinations; every one renders, no panic, no I/O). (adapter-http-viewer) Thread `store.count_countered_own_claims().ok()` (Result → Option, the slice-17 ADR-054 D2 per-count independent degrade) into `landing_page`'s LandingSummary build (the 4th `.ok()` resolution), call `render_landing(&summary)`, always 200. (xtask) UNCHANGED at this step — a read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge, `check_viewer_capability_boundary` + `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` stay GREEN. Outcome: a seeded store (12 own, 3 countered by peers, one of them by TWO distinct peers proving presence-once → count is 3, not 4) + GET / returns a 200 page showing "12 own claims (3 countered)" — the own-claims "12" unchanged beside the genuine seeded presence count. BUILD-BEFORE-RUN (mirrors slice-06..17): the AT spawns the openlore (viewer) bin via ViewerServer::start, so the run MUST cargo build the bin before executing these ATs. NO second binary (the count is a LOCAL DuckDB COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregate). The seed (seed_landing_store_with_countered_own_claims, with the direct ADR-055 read_countered_own_claims_count oracle) + asserts (assert_landing_countered_count, assert_landing_shows_count) already compile in support/mod.rs (they drive the EXISTING claim add/peer add/peer pull verbs + scan strings).
criteria: GET / over a REAL local store seeded (production `claim add` + `peer add` + `peer pull` paths, Pillar 3) with 12 own claims, 3 of them countered by peers (one by TWO distinct peers, proving presence-once → the count is 3, not 4) returns a 200 page showing the own-claims count "12" UNCHANGED and the countered count "(3 countered)" beside it.
StoreReadPort gains a single read-only method `count_countered_own_claims(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>`; NO mutation method is added to the port (ADR-030 / C-1).

DuckDbStoreReadAdapter impls it as ONE aggregate `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.cid) FROM claims c WHERE c.cid IN (SELECT referenced_cid FROM claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters' UNION SELECT referenced_cid FROM peer_claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters')` over the shared connection — a presence count (de-duped IN-set + DISTINCT, no JOIN-fanout), own-only by query shape, parameter-free; the xtask `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` rule stays GREEN by construction (ADR-055 Enforcement).

viewer-domain adds the FOURTH `countered_own_claims: Option<usize>` field to LandingSummary (additive, parallel degrade, ADR-055 D2) and the shared `render_countered(Option<usize>) -> String` helper (Some(n) → "(n countered)", Some(0) → "(0 countered)", None → "(— countered)" via MISSING_COUNT_MARKER, neutral copy); render_landing renders it BESIDE the UNCHANGED own-claims line (additive, never a re-weight, C-4) as a TOTAL function of the summary.

adapter-http-viewer threads `store.count_countered_own_claims().ok()` (the 4th independent `.ok()` resolution, ADR-054 D2 extended) into landing_page's LandingSummary, calls render_landing(&summary), and always returns 200; xtask check-arch stays GREEN (the read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge).
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a REAL local store seeded (production `claim add` + `peer add` + `peer pull` paths, Pillar 3) with 12 own claims, 3 of them countered by peers (one by TWO distinct peers, proving presence-once → the count is 3, not 4) returns a 200 page showing the own-claims count "12" UNCHANGED and the countered count "(3 countered)" beside it.
StoreReadPort gains a single read-only method `count_countered_own_claims(&self) -> Result<usize, StoreReadError>`; NO mutation method is added to the port (ADR-030 / C-1).

DuckDbStoreReadAdapter impls it as ONE aggregate `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.cid) FROM claims c WHERE c.cid IN (SELECT referenced_cid FROM claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters' UNION SELECT referenced_cid FROM peer_claim_references WHERE ref_type='counters')` over the shared connection — a presence count (de-duped IN-set + DISTINCT, no JOIN-fanout), own-only by query shape, parameter-free; the xtask `no_cross_table_join_elides_author` rule stays GREEN by construction (ADR-055 Enforcement).

viewer-domain adds the FOURTH `countered_own_claims: Option<usize>` field to LandingSummary (additive, parallel degrade, ADR-055 D2) and the shared `render_countered(Option<usize>) -> String` helper (Some(n) → "(n countered)", Some(0) → "(0 countered)", None → "(— countered)" via MISSING_COUNT_MARKER, neutral copy); render_landing renders it BESIDE the UNCHANGED own-claims line (additive, never a re-weight, C-4) as a TOTAL function of the summary.

adapter-http-viewer threads `store.count_countered_own_claims().ok()` (the 4th independent `.ok()` resolution, ADR-054 D2 extended) into landing_page's LandingSummary, calls render_landing(&summary), and always returns 200; xtask check-arch stays GREEN (the read-only count method changes no crate-dependency edge).
steps.mutation_note: Adds the load-bearing pure-core surface (the 4th LandingSummary field + the shared render_countered helper rendering Some(n)→"(n countered)" / Some(0)→"(0 countered)" / None→"(— countered)" + the render_landing render beside the unchanged own-claims line) and the count-only read seam (count_countered_own_claims). Extend nightly mutation testing to the render_countered branch (Some(n) renders the number, None renders the marker), the COUNT(DISTINCT) presence aggregate (the de-duped UNION IN-set so a twice-countered claim counts once), and the additive render (the own-claims "12" is never re-weighted by the countered count).

steps.name: The /claims header shows the SAME countered count via render_countered (single source), and a twice-countered claim counts once (presence)
steps.description: Close the single-source + presence-once contracts (CC-HEADER, US-CC-002 Theme 1 Ex 2 / WD-CC-8 + CC-PRESENCE, US-CC-000/001 Theme 2 / C-4 / BR-CC-1 CARDINAL). (CC-HEADER) Extend the `/claims` header to render the SAME countered count: `render_claims_page` gains a `countered_own_claims: Option<usize>` parameter (the SAME Option the landing uses, ADR-055 D3), and renders `render_countered(countered_own_claims)` near the "My Claims" h1 / read-only notice; `claims_page` (adapter-http-viewer) resolves `store.count_countered_own_claims().ok()` and passes it in. Both surfaces resolve from the SAME read method + render through the SAME helper (single source — not a cached value), so the landing "(N countered)" and the `/claims` header "(N countered)" cannot diverge; pinned EQUAL by assert_landing_and_claims_countered_consistent. The `render_claims_page` signature change ripples to its callers/tests (accepted, ADR-055 consequence). (CC-PRESENCE) Over a store where ONE own claim is countered by TWO distinct peers and it is the only countered claim, GET / shows "(1 countered)", NEVER "(2 countered)" — the COUNT(DISTINCT) over the de-duped UNION IN-set collapses the two distinct-author counters of the SAME own CID to ONE membership (C-4 / BR-CC-1), and assert_countered_copy_is_neutral confirms no "disputed by N" sum. Confirmatory of the 01-01 aggregate + helper once they exist; pins the single-source consistency + the presence-once de-dup end-to-end.
criteria: GET /claims over the SAME store (12 own, 3 countered) renders "(3 countered)" in the list header via render_countered — render_claims_page gains a `countered_own_claims: Option<usize>` param, claims_page resolves `count_countered_own_claims().ok()` and passes it in (ADR-055 D3).

The landing "(N countered)" == the /claims header "(N countered)" for the same store (single source — the read method + render helper, not a cached value; assert_landing_and_claims_countered_consistent, WD-CC-8).

Over a store where one own claim is countered by TWO distinct peers (the only countered claim), GET / shows "(1 countered)", NEVER "(2 countered)" — the de-duped UNION IN-set + COUNT(DISTINCT) presence count (C-4 / BR-CC-1 CARDINAL).
The countered copy is neutral disputed-claim awareness — no "disputed by N" sum of counters, no verdict/penalty language (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral).
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims over the SAME store (12 own, 3 countered) renders "(3 countered)" in the list header via render_countered — render_claims_page gains a `countered_own_claims: Option<usize>` param, claims_page resolves `count_countered_own_claims().ok()` and passes it in (ADR-055 D3).

The landing "(N countered)" == the /claims header "(N countered)" for the same store (single source — the read method + render helper, not a cached value; assert_landing_and_claims_countered_consistent, WD-CC-8).

Over a store where one own claim is countered by TWO distinct peers (the only countered claim), GET / shows "(1 countered)", NEVER "(2 countered)" — the de-duped UNION IN-set + COUNT(DISTINCT) presence count (C-4 / BR-CC-1 CARDINAL).
The countered copy is neutral disputed-claim awareness — no "disputed by N" sum of counters, no verdict/penalty language (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral).
steps.mutation_note: The single-source render (BOTH surfaces through render_countered, the /claims header param threaded the SAME `.ok()` value) and the presence-once de-dup (a claim countered by 2 peers counts ONCE) are load-bearing; mutation-test the render_claims_page header render + the COUNT(DISTINCT)/UNION so a mutant that diverges the two surfaces' copy, drops the DISTINCT (rendering "(2 countered)"), or swaps UNION for UNION ALL in a counting context is killed.

steps.name: An honest zero "(0 countered)" on the landing AND in the /claims header (Some(0), distinct from the missing marker; list renders as slice-06)
steps.description: The honest-zero edge on both surfaces (CC-ZERO-LANDING, US-CC-001 Theme 3 / C-5 + CC-ZERO-HEADER, US-CC-002 Theme 3 / C-5 — the success side of `0 ≠ missing`). Over a store with 12 own claims, NONE countered (seed_landing_store_none_countered → the count read returns Ok(0) → Some(0)): (CC-ZERO-LANDING) GET / shows "12 own claims (0 countered)" — Some(0) renders "(0 countered)", a SUCCESSFUL read of zero, DISTINCT from the missing-number marker "(— countered)" and not an omitted count. (CC-ZERO-HEADER) GET /claims shows "(0 countered)" in the header AND renders its rows with NO per-row Countered flag, exactly as slice-06 (nothing is countered → the slice-12 presence read is empty). Confirmatory once 01-01/01-02 exist: the Some(0) → "(0 countered)" render branch is exercised on both surfaces, and the assertion that "(— countered)" is ABSENT pins the success side of the type-level `0 ≠ missing` distinction (WD-CC-6; the failure side is pinned by 02-01 / 03-01).
criteria: GET / over a store with 12 own claims and NONE countered (seed_landing_store_none_countered → the count read returns Some(0)) shows "12 own claims (0 countered)" — Some(0) renders "(0 countered)", a SUCCESSFUL read of zero (assert_landing_countered_count(body, 0)).
The missing marker "(— countered)" is ABSENT on the landing — an honest empty-counter store is a successful read of 0, NOT a failed read (the success side of `0 ≠ missing`, C-5 / WD-CC-6).
GET /claims over the same none-countered store shows "(0 countered)" in the header (assert_claims_header_countered_count(body, 0)).

The /claims list renders its rows with NO per-row Countered flag, exactly as slice-06 (the slice-12 presence read is empty when nothing is countered) — the header "(0 countered)" is the only countered text.
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a store with 12 own claims and NONE countered (seed_landing_store_none_countered → the count read returns Some(0)) shows "12 own claims (0 countered)" — Some(0) renders "(0 countered)", a SUCCESSFUL read of zero (assert_landing_countered_count(body, 0)).
The missing marker "(— countered)" is ABSENT on the landing — an honest empty-counter store is a successful read of 0, NOT a failed read (the success side of `0 ≠ missing`, C-5 / WD-CC-6).
GET /claims over the same none-countered store shows "(0 countered)" in the header (assert_claims_header_countered_count(body, 0)).

The /claims list renders its rows with NO per-row Countered flag, exactly as slice-06 (the slice-12 presence read is empty when nothing is countered) — the header "(0 countered)" is the only countered text.
steps.mutation_note: The Some(0) → "(0 countered)" (not the missing marker) render branch on both surfaces is the success side of `0 ≠ missing`; mutation-test render_countered so a mutant that renders Some(0) as "(— countered)" (or blanks the zero), or one that leaks a per-row Countered flag when the presence set is empty, is killed.

phases.name: The edges — missing≠zero degrade (new cfg-gated fault seam + xtask guard token), additive no-regression, read-only, offline, no-N+1, anti-misread

steps.name: A failed countered-count read degrades to "(— countered)" on BOTH surfaces without a 5xx (the genuinely-new cfg-gated fault seam + xtask release-gate guard token)
steps.description: The one step carrying genuinely-NEW fault-injection work (CC-DEGRADE-LANDING + CC-DEGRADE-HEADER, US-CC-000/001/002 Theme 4 / C-2 / C-5 CARDINAL — the per-count independent missing≠zero degrade). Maria's countered-count read fails mid-request while the own-claims count + the list read still succeed. (CC-DEGRADE-LANDING) GET / returns a NORMAL 200, the own-claims count + the nav hub still render, and the countered count renders the missing marker "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated "(0 countered)"), no raw stack trace. (CC-DEGRADE-HEADER) GET /claims returns a NORMAL 200, the header renders "(— countered)", and the list rows still render ("My Claims" present) — the countered-count read is INDEPENDENT of the list read (ADR-055 D4). The per-count independent degrade: each count is resolved by its OWN `.ok()` (Result → Option), so a failed `count_countered_own_claims` maps to None → render_countered(None) → marker while the siblings stay Some(_) → number (ADR-055 D4; the slice-17 ADR-054 D2 precedent extended to a fourth count). DELIVER materializes the test-only effect-shell fault seam `start_viewer_with_failing_countered_count` → the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT` env var, MIRRORING slice-17's `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT`: a `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`-gated branch substituting `Err(StoreReadError)` for the real `count_countered_own_claims()` on BOTH the `/` and `/claims` handlers so the SAME production `.ok()` degrade branch runs, plus the `#[cfg(not(debug_assertions))]` release identity (NO env-var read compiled in). The DISTILL harness already references `start_viewer_with_failing_countered_count` + the `start_inner` 7th param (a todo!() RED scaffold until DELIVER materializes it). (xtask) Add the NEW release-gate guard token: extend the seam-guard scan (check_arch.rs ~1029-1049, mirroring `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT`) to ALSO cover the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT` token so an UNGATED read in a release build fails check-arch — the slice-16/17 tokens stay, the slice-18 one is added. This pins Earned Trust on the store-read dependency: the design exercises the substrate LYING (a transient StoreReadError) and proves BOTH orientation surfaces survive it without 5xx and without fabricating 0.
criteria: GET / against a viewer whose countered-count read is forced to FAIL mid-request (start_viewer_with_failing_countered_count → OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT; own + other reads still succeed) returns a NORMAL 200 — never a 5xx (C-2 CARDINAL / NFR-VIEW-6); the own-claims count + the nav hub still render, and the countered count renders "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated "(0 countered)"); no raw stack trace leaks.

GET /claims against the same failing viewer returns a NORMAL 200, the header renders "(— countered)" (assert_claims_header_countered_missing), and the list rows still render ("My Claims" present) — the countered-count read is INDEPENDENT of the list read (ADR-055 D4).

DELIVER materializes the test-only `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT` seam behind `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` on BOTH the `/` and `/claims` handlers (release identity is a no-op, mirroring slice-17's OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT), threaded via the `start_inner` 7th param; the per-count `.ok()` degrade (None → render_countered(None) → marker) is the production path under test.

The xtask release-gate seam-guard scan is extended to ALSO cover the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT` token (an UNGATED read fails check-arch); the slice-16/17 tokens stay and the slice-18 token is added (check_arch.rs ~1029-1049).
acceptance_criteria: GET / against a viewer whose countered-count read is forced to FAIL mid-request (start_viewer_with_failing_countered_count → OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT; own + other reads still succeed) returns a NORMAL 200 — never a 5xx (C-2 CARDINAL / NFR-VIEW-6); the own-claims count + the nav hub still render, and the countered count renders "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated "(0 countered)"); no raw stack trace leaks.

GET /claims against the same failing viewer returns a NORMAL 200, the header renders "(— countered)" (assert_claims_header_countered_missing), and the list rows still render ("My Claims" present) — the countered-count read is INDEPENDENT of the list read (ADR-055 D4).

DELIVER materializes the test-only `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT` seam behind `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` on BOTH the `/` and `/claims` handlers (release identity is a no-op, mirroring slice-17's OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_PEER_CLAIMS_COUNT), threaded via the `start_inner` 7th param; the per-count `.ok()` degrade (None → render_countered(None) → marker) is the production path under test.

The xtask release-gate seam-guard scan is extended to ALSO cover the `OPENLORE_VIEWER_FAIL_COUNTERED_COUNT` token (an UNGATED read fails check-arch); the slice-16/17 tokens stay and the slice-18 token is added (check_arch.rs ~1029-1049).
steps.mutation_note: The per-count `.ok()` degrade (failed countered read → None → render_countered(None) → "(— countered)" on BOTH surfaces while the siblings stay Some(_) → number, never a 5xx, never a fabricated 0, and the /claims list rows still render) is load-bearing no-crash logic; mutation-test it so a mutant that fabricates 0 on failure, blanks the sibling counts / list rows, or propagates a 5xx is killed. The `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`-gated seam + the extended xtask guard token are the release-safety enforcement (the seam never ships).

steps.name: The countered count never re-weights the own-claims count (additive) and never re-orders / filters / re-weights the /claims list (byte-identical baseline)
steps.description: The additive / no-regression edges (CC-NO-REWEIGHT, US-CC-001 Theme 5 / C-4 + CC-NO-REORDER, US-CC-002 Theme 5 / C-4 / WD-CC-9). (CC-NO-REWEIGHT) Over a store with 12 own claims, 3 countered, GET / renders the own-claims count "12" EXACTLY (the countered count is additive awareness, never a deduction — the number is NOT 12 - 3 = 9), and the copy carries no penalty/score/"refuted"/"false" language (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral). (CC-NO-REORDER) Over the slice-12 mixed-pages fixture (a mix of countered + un-countered own claims spanning the page, with a known countered subset ≥ 1 pinned by the read_countered_own_claims_count oracle so the header count is falsifiable / no vacuous pass), GET /claims renders the additive header count AND the list order (`composed_at DESC, cid`), page boundaries, total count, and every row's verbatim confidence + the slice-12 per-row flags are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-header-count baseline (the countered rows are not pulled to the top or grouped). REUSES the slice-12 byte-identity assert (which elides the additive `Countered` row anchors; the header "(N countered)" lives OUTSIDE the row body). Confirmatory once 01-01/01-02 exist; pins that the additive header text re-ordered / filtered / re-weighted NOTHING (C-4 / WD-CC-9).
criteria: GET / over a store with 12 own claims, 3 countered, renders the own-claims count "12" EXACTLY (additive, never a deduction — the body never shows "9 own claims"); the front door carries no penalty/score/"refuted"/"false" language (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral).

GET /claims over the slice-12 mixed-pages fixture (known countered subset ≥ 1, pinned by the read_countered_own_claims_count oracle so the header count is falsifiable) RENDERS the additive header count (assert_claims_header_countered_count(expected)) — the feature is present, killing a vacuous pass.

The /claims list order (`composed_at DESC, cid`), page boundaries, total count, and every row's verbatim confidence are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-header-count slice-06 baseline (assert_list_order_and_confidence_byte_identical) — the countered rows are not pulled to the top or grouped (C-4 / WD-CC-9).
The header count is additive header text living OUTSIDE the row body — it re-ordered / filtered / re-paged / re-weighted NOTHING in the list.
acceptance_criteria: GET / over a store with 12 own claims, 3 countered, renders the own-claims count "12" EXACTLY (additive, never a deduction — the body never shows "9 own claims"); the front door carries no penalty/score/"refuted"/"false" language (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral).

GET /claims over the slice-12 mixed-pages fixture (known countered subset ≥ 1, pinned by the read_countered_own_claims_count oracle so the header count is falsifiable) RENDERS the additive header count (assert_claims_header_countered_count(expected)) — the feature is present, killing a vacuous pass.

The /claims list order (`composed_at DESC, cid`), page boundaries, total count, and every row's verbatim confidence are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the no-header-count slice-06 baseline (assert_list_order_and_confidence_byte_identical) — the countered rows are not pulled to the top or grouped (C-4 / WD-CC-9).
The header count is additive header text living OUTSIDE the row body — it re-ordered / filtered / re-paged / re-weighted NOTHING in the list.
steps.mutation_note: The additive-no-regression property (the own-claims "12" is never deducted by the countered count; the /claims list order/paging/total/confidence + the slice-12 per-row flags are byte-identical to the no-header-count baseline) is load-bearing; mutation-test render_landing + render_claims_page so a mutant that deducts the countered count from the own-claims number, or one that re-orders / filters / groups the list by countered presence, is killed.

steps.name: Read-only, offline, no-N+1, anti-misread on both surfaces — the count is render-only LOCAL text, neutral, invariant to store size
steps.description: The remaining cross-cutting story edges that flip green for free once 01-01..02-02 exist (CC-READONLY-LANDING/HEADER, Theme 6 / C-1 CARDINAL; CC-OFFLINE-LANDING/HEADER, Theme 7 / C-2 CARDINAL; CC-NO-N-PLUS-1, Theme 8 / C-3 CARDINAL; CC-ANTI-MISREAD, Theme 9 / C-6 / WD-CC-10). (CC-READONLY-*) GET / and GET /claims over a seeded counter-aware store render the countered count AND carry NO `<form>`/`<button>`/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — the countered count is render-only text, never a sort/filter/mutating control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control; C-1 CARDINAL, the slice-06 no-key guarantee inherited). (CC-OFFLINE-*) Both surfaces render the countered count fully network-down (the count is a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregate with NO outbound edge — offline-STRONGER than /search/scrape) and reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() false; C-2 / KPI-HX-G2). (CC-NO-N-PLUS-1) A LARGE store (the 3 named countered own claims + 120 more PLAIN own claims via seed_own_claims_via_cli, the countered count pinned STILL 3 by the read_countered_own_claims_count oracle) renders the right count (3) in ONE request — the aggregate read returns the right total invariant to store size, no per-claim counter-presence loop (the strict 1-query bound is a DELIVER adapter-duckdb unit test). (CC-ANTI-MISREAD) Over the one-own-claim-countered-twice fixture (confidence 0.30), GET / shows the neutral presence count "(1 countered)" (never "(2 countered)"), and the copy is never "refuted"/"false"/"disputed by 2"/a score/a deduction (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral). Confirmatory once the render + aggregate + helper exist; pins read-only / offline / no-N+1 / neutral-copy on the OBSERVABLE surfaces.
criteria: GET / and GET /claims over a seeded counter-aware store render the countered count AND carry NO `<form>`/`<button>`/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — the count is render-only text, never a sort/filter/mutating control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control; C-1 CARDINAL).

Both surfaces render the countered count fully network-down (a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregate, NO outbound edge) and reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js — references_external_cdn() is false (C-2 / KPI-HX-G2).

A LARGE store (3 countered own claims + 120 more plain own claims; the countered count pinned STILL 3 by the read_countered_own_claims_count oracle) renders "(3 countered)" in ONE request — the aggregate read is invariant to store size, no per-claim loop (C-3 CARDINAL).

Over the one-own-claim-countered-twice fixture (confidence 0.30), GET / shows the neutral presence count "(1 countered)" (never "(2 countered)"), and the copy is never "refuted"/"false"/"disputed by 2"/a score/a deduction (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral; C-6 / WD-CC-10).
acceptance_criteria: GET / and GET /claims over a seeded counter-aware store render the countered count AND carry NO `<form>`/`<button>`/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — the count is render-only text, never a sort/filter/mutating control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control; C-1 CARDINAL).

Both surfaces render the countered count fully network-down (a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregate, NO outbound edge) and reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js — references_external_cdn() is false (C-2 / KPI-HX-G2).

A LARGE store (3 countered own claims + 120 more plain own claims; the countered count pinned STILL 3 by the read_countered_own_claims_count oracle) renders "(3 countered)" in ONE request — the aggregate read is invariant to store size, no per-claim loop (C-3 CARDINAL).

Over the one-own-claim-countered-twice fixture (confidence 0.30), GET / shows the neutral presence count "(1 countered)" (never "(2 countered)"), and the copy is never "refuted"/"false"/"disputed by 2"/a score/a deduction (assert_countered_copy_is_neutral; C-6 / WD-CC-10).
steps.mutation_note: The render-only-ness (no mutating control), offline (no outbound edge / vendored-only htmx), no-N+1 (one aggregate read invariant to store size), and neutral anti-misread copy are load-bearing on both surfaces; mutation-test render_landing/render_claims_page/render_countered so a mutant that wraps the count in a control, adds a CDN reference, introduces a per-claim loop, or emits verdict/penalty/"disputed by N" copy is killed.

phases.name: Gold guardrail invariants (countered count — read-only, no-write, offline-chrome, offline, no-N+1, missing≠zero, presence-once, single-source, no-regression)

steps.name: Gold: the nine countered-count guardrail invariants (read-only, no-write, offline-chrome, offline, no-N+1, missing≠zero, presence-once, single-source, no-regression)
steps.description: The nine cross-cutting GOLD guardrails (CC-INV-*) that must hold over the WHOLE countered-count surface on `GET /` + `GET /claims` — the BEHAVIORAL layer of the three-layer ADR-055 enforcement (type [StoreReadPort declares no mutation method; count_countered_own_claims returns Result<usize, _>] + xtask [check_viewer_capability_boundary + no_cross_table_join_elides_author, GREEN by construction] are the other two). They drive the REAL openlore ui subprocess + HTTP over a REAL seeded LOCAL DuckDB, no mocked boundary (the count is a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) read + PURE render). (CC-INV-ReadOnly, C-1 / WD-CC-1) exercising GET / + GET /claims over a seeded store leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED (universe-bound assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe; the countered count is computed per request and persists nothing). (CC-INV-NoWrite, C-1 CARDINAL) neither GET / nor GET /claims renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control — the count is render-only text (assert_landing_read_only_no_control on both). (CC-INV-OfflineChrome, C-2 / KPI-HX-G2) both pages reference ONLY the local /static/htmx.min.js, NO CDN (references_external_cdn() false). (CC-INV-Offline, C-2 / KPI-5) both surfaces render the countered count fully network-down (a LOCAL read with NO outbound edge). (CC-INV-NoNPlus1, C-3 CARDINAL) a LARGE store (200 extra plain own claims; the countered count pinned STILL 3 by the oracle) renders the right count in ONE request — the read count is invariant to store size. (CC-INV-MissingNotZero, C-2 / C-5 / WD-CC-6 CARDINAL) BOTH sides: a none-countered store renders "(0 countered)" (NOT the marker) while a failed countered read (via the 02-01 fault seam) renders "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated 0) — a fabricated 0 on failure is unrepresentable (the shell maps a failed read to None, never Some(0)). (CC-INV-PresenceOnce, C-4 / BR-CC-1 CARDINAL) a claim countered by TWO distinct peers contributes ONCE — BOTH the landing and the /claims header render "(1 countered)", never "(2 countered)". (CC-INV-SingleSource, WD-CC-8) the landing "(N countered)" == the /claims header "(N countered)" for the same store. (CC-INV-NoRegression, C-4 / WD-CC-9) the /claims list order/paging/count/confidence + the slice-12 per-row flags are byte-identical to the no-header-count baseline. These INHERIT the slice-06/17 viewer golds (viewer_is_read_only / store_views_work_offline / web_process_holds_no_signing_key / the slice-17 landing golds) and add the COUNTERED-COUNT specifics. Flip green for free once 01-01..02-03 exist (the MissingNotZero failure side reuses the 02-01 fault seam). LAST roadmap step.
criteria: CC-INV-ReadOnly + CC-INV-NoWrite: exercising GET / + GET /claims over a seeded store leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe), and neither response renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control on both) — the count is render-only text, the viewer holds no key (C-1 CARDINAL / WD-CC-1).

CC-INV-OfflineChrome + CC-INV-Offline: both pages reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() false), AND both surfaces render the countered count fully network-down with NO outbound edge (a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) read; C-2 / KPI-HX-G2 / KPI-5).

CC-INV-NoNPlus1 + CC-INV-MissingNotZero + CC-INV-PresenceOnce: a LARGE store (200 extra plain own claims, countered count pinned STILL 3) renders the right count in ONE request invariant to store size; the none-countered store renders "(0 countered)" (NOT the marker) while the failed-read path (02-01 seam) renders "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated 0) — both sides of `0 ≠ missing`; and a claim countered by TWO peers renders "(1 countered)" on BOTH surfaces, never "(2 countered)".

CC-INV-SingleSource + CC-INV-NoRegression: the landing "(N countered)" == the /claims header "(N countered)" for the same store (assert_landing_and_claims_countered_consistent, WD-CC-8); and the /claims list order/paging/count/confidence + the slice-12 per-row flags are byte-identical to the no-header-count baseline (assert_list_order_and_confidence_byte_identical, C-4 / WD-CC-9).
acceptance_criteria: CC-INV-ReadOnly + CC-INV-NoWrite: exercising GET / + GET /claims over a seeded store leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe), and neither response renders a write/compose/sign/subscribe/follow control (assert_landing_read_only_no_control on both) — the count is render-only text, the viewer holds no key (C-1 CARDINAL / WD-CC-1).

CC-INV-OfflineChrome + CC-INV-Offline: both pages reference ONLY the vendored local /static/htmx.min.js (references_external_cdn() false), AND both surfaces render the countered count fully network-down with NO outbound edge (a LOCAL COUNT(DISTINCT) read; C-2 / KPI-HX-G2 / KPI-5).

CC-INV-NoNPlus1 + CC-INV-MissingNotZero + CC-INV-PresenceOnce: a LARGE store (200 extra plain own claims, countered count pinned STILL 3) renders the right count in ONE request invariant to store size; the none-countered store renders "(0 countered)" (NOT the marker) while the failed-read path (02-01 seam) renders "(— countered)" (NOT a fabricated 0) — both sides of `0 ≠ missing`; and a claim countered by TWO peers renders "(1 countered)" on BOTH surfaces, never "(2 countered)".

CC-INV-SingleSource + CC-INV-NoRegression: the landing "(N countered)" == the /claims header "(N countered)" for the same store (assert_landing_and_claims_countered_consistent, WD-CC-8); and the /claims list order/paging/count/confidence + the slice-12 per-row flags are byte-identical to the no-header-count baseline (assert_list_order_and_confidence_byte_identical, C-4 / WD-CC-9).
steps.mutation_note: These nine guardrails are the behavioral layer of the ADR-055 three-layer enforcement; ensure mutation testing of the pure render core (render_countered Some(n)→"(n countered)" / Some(0)→"(0 countered)" / None→"(— countered)", the additive own-claims render, no-control, no-CDN, neutral copy) + the COUNT(DISTINCT) presence aggregate + the per-count `.ok()` degrade is green so the gold guardrails are not the only line of defense. The presence-once (count(distinct)), single-source (landing==header), no-regression (byte-identical list), missing≠zero (both sides), and no-N+1 (invariant to store size) are the countered-count-specific additions over the inherited slice-06/17 golds.

validation.note: APPROVED — all dimensions PASS; 7 steps/24 scenarios
